title: Paths, Navigation, and Filesystem Hierarchy · Terminal Checkpoint
summary: Locate application configuration and logs on a workstation whose current directory and distribution conventions differ from a familiar machine. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.
Use pwd, cd, and directory stacks while predicting the resulting working directory before each transition.
Relate root-level directories to boot, configuration, devices, variable data, user data, runtime state, and optional software roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Absolute Relative Paths · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

Which action demonstrates Absolute Relative Paths without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.

content.text: On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.

content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Absolute Relative Paths result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide — Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A path without a leading slash always starts from the home directory.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Path Portability: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — On a quiz host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose paths from documented interfaces and discovered configuration rather than hard-coded workstation assumptions.
